checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is bit_reversal_permutation applied BEFORE inverse FFT in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the polynomial_eval_to_coeff implementation in the target client. 2. Verify bit_reversal_permutation is called before inverse FFT. 3. Create a test blob and verify coefficient representation matches expected output. 4. Compare coefficients with a reference implementation to detect ordering errors.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-13, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Omitting or reordering bit-reversal produces incorrect coefficients corrupting all KZG proofs.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does multiply_polynomialcoeff (SG-009) assert polynomial degree bounds before multiplication?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate multiply_polynomialcoeff implementation. 2. Verify assertion: len(a) + len(b) <= FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 3. Craft inputs exceeding bounds and verify rejection. 4. Verify product polynomial degree equals deg(a) + deg(b).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-15, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Exceeding degree bound produces polynomials outside trusted setup support.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does interpolate_polynomialcoeff (SG-011) reject duplicate x-values?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate interpolate_polynomialcoeff implementation. 2. Verify duplicate x-value rejection or crash on division by zero. 3. Submit batch verification with duplicate cell_indices. 4. Verify duplicate x-values cause rejection before reaching interpolation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-18, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Duplicate x-values cause division by zero in weight_adjustment computation.

checklist.title: Verify KZG Proof Generation Correctness: Is quotient polynomial Q(X) computed correctly in compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl implementation. 2. Verify Q(X) = (f(X) - I(X)) / Z(X) computation. 3. Verify I(X) correctly interpolates f at evaluation points. 4. Verify proof is commitment to Q(X) using KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL. 5. Compare proof with reference implementation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-22, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-004. Incorrect proof generation breaks data availability guarantee.

checklist.title: Verify Quotient Polynomial Degree Bounds: Does compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (SG-014) enforce trusted setup bounds?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify quotient degree check: deg(Q) = deg(f) - len(zs). 2. Verify failure when deg(Q) >= len(KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL). 3. Craft recovery inputs producing high-degree polynomials. 4. Verify proof generation fails explicitly, not silently.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-23, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Exceeding trusted setup bounds produces invalid commitments.

checklist.title: Verify Batch Verification Pairing Equation: Is verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) pairing check correct?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify pairing equation: e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]). 2. Verify LL = g1_lincomb(proofs, r_powers). 3. Verify LR = KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n]. 4. Verify RL = RLC - RLI + RLP. 5. Attempt to forge proofs and verify rejection. 6. Verify ALL cells in batch are verified.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-24, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006, tb-007. Broken verification allows forged data to be accepted.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (SG-015) validate commitment_indices?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify no duplicate commitment entries in commitment_indices. 2. Verify all commitment_indices[k] < len(commitments). 3. Submit out-of-bounds commitment_indices and verify rejection. 4. Submit duplicate commitments and verify detection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-25, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Out-of-bounds access causes crash, duplicates manipulate weight aggregation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016) validate cell_index bounds?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 2. Submit cell_index >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB and verify rejection. 3. Verify returned value is root of unity from bit-reversal-permuted extended domain.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-26, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Out-of-bounds cell_index causes memory access beyond roots array.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does compute_cells (SG-018) validate blob size and produce correct cell count?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB. 2. Submit blob with incorrect size and verify rejection. 3. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells produced. 4. Verify each cell represents polynomial evaluation over distinct coset.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-28, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002. Missing blob length validation allows malformed blobs.

checklist.title: Verify Cell-Proof Alignment: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) produce aligned cell-proof pairs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cells and proofs produced. 2. Verify each proof is valid for corresponding cell and coset. 3. Use verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch to validate all pairs. 4. Test with various blob inputs to detect misalignment.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-29, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-004. Cell-proof misalignment causes all sidecars to fail verification.

checklist.title: Verify Public Entry Point Validation: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-020) validate blob size?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(blob) == BYTES_PER_BLOB at entry. 2. Submit arbitrary-size blobs and verify rejection. 3. Verify output matches blob_to_polynomial + polynomial_eval_to_coeff + compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff sequence.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-30, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Public entry point must validate before processing.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) validate ALL input constraints?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify all four input lists have equal length. 2. Verify each commitment has exactly BYTES_PER_COMMITMENT bytes. 3. Verify each cell_index < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Verify each cell has exactly BYTES_PER_CELL bytes. 5. Verify each proof has exactly BYTES_PER_PROOF bytes. 6. Submit malformed inputs for each constraint and verify rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-31, Trust Boundary: tb-007. All inputs are untrusted from P2P peers.

checklist.title: Verify Commitment Deduplication Correctness: Does verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021) preserve semantic equivalence?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit verification batch with duplicate commitments. 2. Verify each cell is verified against its original commitment. 3. Compare deduplication results with non-deduplicated verification. 4. Submit duplicate commitments with different cell data and verify correct index mapping.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-32, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Incorrect deduplication maps cells to wrong commitments.

checklist.title: Can the vanishing polynomial construction (SG-022) be falsified with specific missing cell patterns?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify vanishing polynomial evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points. 2. Verify vanishing polynomial is nonzero at all present cell evaluation points. 3. Test coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL. 4. Create various missing cell patterns and verify polynomial correctness.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-33. Incorrect vanishing polynomial corrupts recovery.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) satisfy algebraic identity?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over FFT domain. 2. Verify division by Z(x) is performed over coset. 3. Submit consistent cells and verify correct recovery. 4. Submit inconsistent cells and verify detection via post-recovery verification.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-34, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009. Incorrect recovery produces wrong cells and proofs.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023) correctly truncate high-degree coefficients?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify truncation to poly_coeff[:FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB]. 2. Verify high-degree coefficients are zero for valid recovery. 3. Submit inconsistent cells producing nonzero high-degree coefficients. 4. Verify such recoveries are detected and rejected.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-35, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Missing truncation validation causes silent data corruption.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024) validate all recovery preconditions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify 50% threshold: require >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells. 2. Submit fewer cells and verify rejection. 3. Verify cell indices are unique, sorted, and < CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 4. Submit duplicate, unsorted, or out-of-bounds indices and verify rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-36, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Missing precondition checks cause undefined recovery behavior.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs (SG-024) verify recovered cells against commitment?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify post-recovery verification using verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch (SG-021). 2. Submit inconsistent cells from different polynomials. 3. Verify such inputs are detected via post-recovery verification. 4. Verify failed verification rejects the recovery.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-37, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009. Missing post-recovery verification allows incorrect data propagation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_validators_custody_requirement (SG-001/validator) correctly clamp values?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify return value in [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS]. 2. Test with minimum effective_balance and verify minimum clamping. 3. Test with maximum effective_balance and verify maximum clamping. 4. Verify min(max(count, VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT), NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS) is correctly applied.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-38, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect clamping allows zero custody groups.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction (SG-002/validator) produce correct cell-proof counts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs per blob. 2. Verify proof slicing: start = i * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB, end = (i + 1) * CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB. 3. Verify cells from compute_cells(blob) align with proofs slice. 4. Submit blob bundle with incorrect proof count and verify detection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-39, Trust Boundary: tb-013. Misaligned proofs cause all sidecars to be rejected.

checklist.title: Verify Sidecar Construction: Does get_data_column_sidecars (SG-003/validator) produce correct column counts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify exactly NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS sidecars produced. 2. Verify each sidecar.index == column_index. 3. Verify each sidecar contains exactly one cell and one proof per blob. 4. Test with various blob counts to detect column construction errors.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-40, Trust Boundary: tb-010. Incorrect column indexing causes coverage gaps.

checklist.title: Verify Merkle Proof Construction: Does get_data_column_sidecars_from_block (SG-004/validator) use correct generalized index?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify Merkle proof targets blob_kzg_commitments field. 2. Verify generalized index is correct for Fulu fork BeaconBlockBody. 3. Validate proofs with verify_merkle_proof. 4. Cross-reference generalized index with SSZ spec for Fulu fork.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-41, Trust Boundary: tb-014. Incorrect generalized index makes all sidecars fail verification.

checklist.title: Verify Subnet Mapping: Is each sidecar published to correct gossip subnet?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(column_index) produces consistent results. 2. Compare subnet mapping across multiple clients. 3. Verify each sidecar is published to exactly one subnet. 4. Identify any client that computes different subnet IDs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-42, Trust Boundary: tb-010. Inconsistent subnet mapping fragments data availability.

checklist.title: Verify Sidecar Retention: Do validators retain sidecars for MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Request sidecars during retention period and verify serving. 2. Measure retention period and compare with MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS. 3. Request sidecars just before retention expires. 4. Identify any client with premature pruning.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-43, Trust Boundary: tb-012. Premature pruning reduces data recovery sources.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are values >= BLS_MODULUS rejected at bytes_to_bls_field?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft blob with byte sequences encoding values >= BLS_MODULUS. 2. Verify such blobs are rejected. 3. Compare behavior across multiple clients. 4. Verify no client silently reduces values modulo BLS_MODULUS.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-44, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-001. Silent modular reduction causes consensus divergence.

checklist.title: Verify Proof Generation Performance: Does compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff (SG-019) meet time budget?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Measure O(n^2) proof generation time for single blob. 2. Measure proof generation for maximum blobs per block. 3. Compare total time with slot time budget. 4. Verify rate-limiting or resource allocation prevents DoS.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-45, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Threat: stride-dos-tb-001. O(n^2) complexity may exceed slot time.

checklist.title: Verify Trusted Setup Documentation: Is trapdoor compromise documented as single point of failure?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review trusted setup ceremony documentation. 2. Verify trapdoor compromise is documented as critical risk. 3. Verify ceremony protocol follows best practices. 4. Review multi-party computation transcript for completeness.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-46, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-004. CONDITIONAL: Trusted setup compromise is foundational assumption. Discovery of ceremony weakness would be eligible.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does batch verification detect tampered evaluation data?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit cells with valid formatting but tampered evaluations. 2. Verify rejection by pairing-based verification. 3. Verify Fiat-Shamir challenge detects any individual cell tampering. 4. Test tampering in various positions within batch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-48, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-007. Batch verification must detect all tampering.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Can proofs be reused across different cells or commitments?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Take valid proof from one (commitment, cell) pair. 2. Attempt to use same proof for different cell or commitment. 3. Verify Fiat-Shamir challenge includes all components. 4. Verify substitution changes challenge r and invalidates verification.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-49, Trust Boundaries: tb-006, tb-007, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-007. Proof reuse allows false data availability claims.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are mutually inconsistent recovery cells detected?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Provide partial cells that are individually valid but from different polynomials. 2. Verify recovery proceeds but post-recovery verification fails. 3. Verify incorrect recovery is not accepted without verification. 4. Identify any client missing post-recovery verification.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-50, Trust Boundaries: tb-008, tb-009, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-008. Inconsistent cells cause wrong polynomial recovery.

checklist.title: Verify Network Resilience: Can selective subnet censorship reduce availability below 50%?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Analyze custody group distribution across subnets. 2. Simulate targeted subnet disruption. 3. Calculate minimum number of subnets to censor for <50% availability. 4. Verify custody redundancy survives targeted censorship.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-51, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Threat: stride-dos-tb-010. Subnet censorship degrades data availability.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is sidecar request rate-limiting implemented?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Flood validator with sidecar requests. 2. Verify rate-limiting per-peer is implemented. 3. Measure resource impact on consensus duties. 4. Identify clients without rate-limiting on sidecar serving.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-52, Trust Boundary: tb-012, Threat: stride-dos-tb-012. Request flooding exhausts validator resources.

checklist.title: Verify Privacy: Can custody group membership be inferred from column serving behavior?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Request all columns from validator and measure response times. 2. Analyze availability patterns between custody and non-custody columns. 3. Identify timing or availability side-channels. 4. Verify response behavior is uniform across all columns.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-53, Trust Boundary: tb-012, Threat: stride-infodisclosure-tb-012. Custody inference enables targeted attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are custody requirements computed from finalized state only?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify custody computation uses finalized or justified state. 2. Create fork scenario with different effective balances. 3. Verify custody assignments remain consistent across fork. 4. Identify any client using non-finalized state for custody.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-54, Trust Boundary: tb-011, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-011. Forked state causes custody divergence.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is blobs_bundle.proofs length validated before slicing?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify assertion: len(proofs) == CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB * len(blobs). 2. Submit blob bundle with incorrect proof count. 3. Verify rejection before proof slicing. 4. Verify no out-of-bounds access during slicing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-55, Trust Boundary: tb-013, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-013. Missing validation causes crash during slicing.

checklist.title: Verify Merkle Proof Generalized Index: Is blob_kzg_commitments index correct for Fulu fork?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify generalized index for blob_kzg_commitments in Fulu BeaconBlockBody. 2. Compare across multiple client implementations. 3. Verify index is updated from pre-Fulu fork. 4. Validate Merkle proofs with correct index.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-56, Trust Boundary: tb-014, Threat: stride-tampering-tb-014. Incorrect index at fork causes network-wide disruption.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does incorrect inverse FFT produce higher-degree polynomials?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify inverse FFT with bit-reversal produces expected degree. 2. Compare coefficient degrees across client implementations. 3. Verify quotient polynomial stays within trusted setup bounds. 4. Test various blob inputs for degree consistency.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-57, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Threat: stride-elevation-tb-003. Higher-degree polynomials exceed trusted setup.

checklist.title: Verify Recovery Performance: Does recovery with minimum cells meet time bounds?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trigger recovery with exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2 cells. 2. Measure recovery time with O(n log n) FFT operations. 3. Trigger repeated recoveries and measure resource impact. 4. Verify rate-limiting on recovery attempts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-58, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Threat: stride-dos-tb-008. Repeated recovery exhausts resources.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is division by zero possible in recovery?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY is correct. 2. Verify coset does not overlap with FFT domain. 3. Verify zp_over_coset contains no zero values. 4. Test various missing cell patterns for division by zero.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-59, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Threat: stride-infodisclosure-tb-008. Incorrect root of unity causes crash.

checklist.title: Verify Data Availability Sampling: Is data withholding detectable?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Simulate data withholding attack. 2. Verify sampling by multiple peers detects withholding. 3. Analyze probability of evasion. 4. Verify economic incentives align with honest behavior.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-60, Trust Boundary: tb-010, Threat: stride-repudiation-tb-010. Successful withholding allows invalid state transitions.

checklist.title: Verify Custody Floor: Are low-balance validators assigned minimum custody groups?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify minimum VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT assignment. 2. Analyze impact of many low-stake validators on coverage. 3. Calculate required attack investment for coverage gaps. 4. Verify floor provides substantial baseline coverage.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-61, Trust Boundary: tb-011, Threat: stride-elevation-tb-011. Low-stake validators may dilute custody coverage.

checklist.title: Verify Bit-Reversal Consistency: Is BRP applied consistently across all code paths?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify BRP in polynomial_eval_to_coeff (SG-007). 2. Verify BRP in coset_shift_for_cell (SG-016). 3. Verify BRP in coset_for_cell (SG-017). 4. Verify BRP in recover_polynomialcoeff (SG-023). 5. Cross-reference all paths for consistency.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-63, Trust Boundaries: tb-003, tb-009. Inconsistent BRP causes intermittent proof failures.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is batch verification resource cost bounded?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Measure resource cost linear with num_cells. 2. Submit very large verification batches. 3. Verify maximum batch size is bounded. 4. Identify clients lacking batch size limits.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-64, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Threat: stride-dos-tb-007. Unbounded batches exhaust CPU.

checklist.title: Verify Fiat-Shamir Hash Security: Is hash_to_bls_field collision-resistant?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify hash function is cryptographically secure. 2. Verify random oracle behavior. 3. Attempt to find two input sets producing same challenge r. 4. Verify hash function is not weak or deprecated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-65, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Threat: stride-spoofing-tb-006. Hash collision enables proof substitution.

checklist.title: Verify Pairing Check Soundness: Does e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1]) reject invalid proofs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify pairing check implementation correctness. 2. Submit invalid KZG proofs and verify rejection. 3. Calculate rejection probability (1 - 1/q). 4. Test pairing library for implementation bugs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-68, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Unsound pairing allows forged proofs.

checklist.title: Verify Cryptographic Assumptions: Is BLS12-381 bilinear Diffie-Hellman assumption maintained?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review cryptographic assumption documentation. 2. Verify BLS12-381 is standard and well-analyzed. 3. Monitor for any mathematical breakthroughs in DLP. 4. Verify implementation uses correct curve parameters.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-69. CONDITIONAL: Foundational assumption. A practical DLP break would be a major mathematical breakthrough.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is total_node_balance computation safe from overflow?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify balance sum is non-negative. 2. Calculate maximum possible sum (validators * max_balance). 3. Verify sum fits in implementation integer type. 4. Test with large validator sets for overflow.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-70, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Overflow produces incorrect custody requirements.

checklist.title: Verify Fork Epoch Ordering: Are fork epoch constants strictly ordered?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify GENESIS_FORK_EPOCH < ALTAIR_FORK_EPOCH < ... < FULU_FORK_EPOCH. 2. Compare fork epoch values across client implementations. 3. Verify compute_fork_version returns correct version for each epoch. 4. Identify any client with mis-ordered constants.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-1, Trust Boundaries: tb-009, tb-010. Mis-ordering causes network-wide consensus split.

checklist.title: Verify Fork Version Function: Does compute_fork_version return exactly one version for any epoch?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify if-else cascade is mutually exclusive and exhaustive. 2. Test every epoch boundary value. 3. Verify exactly one branch is taken for each epoch. 4. Identify any epoch where function fails or returns wrong version.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-2, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Missing branch causes crash or wrong domain separation.

checklist.title: Verify Fork Version Robustness: Does compute_fork_version handle edge-case epochs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test with epoch = 0. 2. Test with epoch = uint64_max. 3. Verify function never returns null, panics, or throws. 4. Craft ENR records with extreme epoch values and test.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W4B13-3, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Edge case epochs in ENR records cause DoS.
